levels.title: The Message
levels.narrative: You've cleaned up the server, but there's a locked file containing a message from the phantom_admin. You need to read its contents to get the next password fragment.
levels.riddle:
A file named 'encrypted_note.txt' holds a key,
Read its contents for all to see.
A feline command will show what's inside —
The password fragment can no longer hide.

files.encrypted_note.txt:
PASSWORD FRAGMENT #3: QUE
The phantom_admin whispers: 'You're getting closer...'
files.decoy_note.txt: This is not the note you're looking for.

hints: You need to display the contents of a file in the terminal.
Think of a small furry animal... the command 'concatenates' files to output.

levels.title: The Inscription
levels.narrative: The server demands a passphrase to unlock the next area. You must write the word 'OPEN_SESAME' into a file called 'passphrase.txt'. The server will check the file's contents.
levels.riddle:
Write your words into a file so new,
'passphrase.txt' awaits your verbal glue.
The word 'OPEN_SESAME' must be stored inside,
Echo your voice, let the text reside.

files.instructions.txt: Write 'OPEN_SESAME' to a file called 'passphrase.txt' to proceed.

hints: You need to create a file with specific text content.
The 'echo' command prints text. The '>' operator redirects output to a file.

levels.title: The Long Scroll
levels.narrative: You've found the phantom_admin's log file. It's hundreds of lines long! The clue you need is on the very last line. You don't need to read the whole thing...
levels.riddle:
A scroll so long, it fills the screen with text,
But only the last line holds what comes next.
Don't read it all — just grab the tail,
The final words will tell the tale.

files.server_log.txt:
Log entry 001: System boot
Log entry 002: Memory check passed
Log entry 003: Disk check passed
Log entry 004: Network initialized
Log entry 005: Running diagnostics
Log entry 006: Loading modules
Log entry 007: User database loaded
Log entry 008: Security scan started
Log entry 009: Firewall rules applied
Log entry 010: Backup system online
Log entry 011: Monitoring services started
Log entry 012: System health: OK
Log entry 013: Scheduled tasks loaded
Log entry 014: Mail server initialized
Log entry 015: Web server started
Log entry 016: Database connection pool ready
Log entry 017: Cache warmed up
Log entry 018: API gateway online
Log entry 019: All services running
Log entry 020: PASSWORD FRAGMENT #4: ST_

hints: You only need the last few lines of a very long file.
The 'tail' command shows the last lines of a file (opposite of 'head').

levels.title: Appending the Log
levels.narrative: The server's authentication log 'auth.log' needs your name added to it. But be careful — you must APPEND to the log, not overwrite it! The existing entries must remain intact.
levels.riddle:
The file 'auth.log' tracks who's been here before,
Add your name without erasing the lore.
One arrow overwrites, but two will append —
Add 'PLAYER_LOGGED_IN' to the very end.

files.auth.log:
phantom_admin logged in: 2003-01-15
root logged in: 2003-06-20
guest logged in: 2003-12-01

hints: You need to ADD text to the end of a file without erasing existing content.
'>' overwrites a file, but '>>' appends to the end.
